volumeTitle: The Woman of Andros. The Self-Tormentor. The Eunuch
label: The Eunuch (Section 5.6.1018 - Section 5.8.1067b)

sections.label: Section 5.6.1018
sections.translation: Yes , indeed , if you can do it with impunity .

sections.label: Section 5.6.1018b
sections.translation: Exactly so .

sections.label: Section 5.6.1018c
sections.translation: By heavens , I ’ ll repay you !

sections.label: Section 5.6.1018d
sections.translation: I believe you ; but , perhaps , that which you are threatening , Parmeno , will need a future day ;

sections.label: Section 5.6.1020
sections.original: Tu iam pendebis , qui stultum adolescentulum nobilitas
sections.translation: you ’ ll be trussed up directly , for rendering a silly young man remarkable for disgraceful conduct , and then betraying him to his father ; they ’ ll both be making an example of you .

sections.label: Section 5.6.1022
sections.translation: I ’ m done for !

sections.label: Section 5.6.1022b
sections.original: Hic pro illo munere tibi honos est habitus : abeo .
sections.translation: This reward has been found you in return for that present of yours ; [*] I ’ m off .

sections.label: Section 5.6.1023
sections.original: Egomet meo indicio miser quasi sorex hodie perii .
sections.translation: Wretch that I am ; just like a rat , this day I ’ ve come to destruction through betrayal of myself . [*]

sections.label: Section 5.7.1024
sections.original: Quid nunc ? qua spe aut quo consilio huc imus ? quid inceptas , Thraso ?
sections.translation: Well now ? With what hope , or what design , are we come hither ? What do you intend to do , Thraso ?

sections.label: Section 5.7.1025
sections.original: Egone ? ut Thaidi me dedam , et faciam quod iubeat .
sections.translation: What , I ? To surrender myself to Thais , and do what she bids me .

sections.label: Section 5.7.1025b
sections.translation: What is it you say ?

sections.label: Section 5.7.1026
sections.original: Qui minus quam Hercules servivit Omphalae ?
sections.translation: Why any the less so , than Hercules served Omphale . [*]

sections.label: Section 5.7.1026b
sections.translation: The precedent pleases me . ( Aside .) I only wish I may see your head stroked down with a slipper ; [*] but her door makes a noise .

sections.label: Section 5.7.1028b
sections.original: Perii . Quid hoc autem est mali ?
sections.translation: Confusion ! Why , what mischiefsthis ? I never saw this person before ; why , I wonder , is he rushing out in such a hurry ?

sections.label: Section 5.8.1030
sections.original: O populares , ecquis me hodie vivit fortunatior ?
sections.translation: O fellow-townsmen , is there any one alive more fortunate than me this day ? Not any one , upon my faith : for clearly in me have the Gods manifested all their power , on whom , thus suddenly , so many blessings are bestowed .

sections.label: Section 5.8.1033
sections.translation: Why is he thus overjoyed ?

sections.label: Section 5.8.1033b
sections.original: O Parmeno mi , O mearum voluptatum omnium
sections.translation: O my dear Parmeno , the contriver , the beginner , the perfecter of all my delights , do you know what are my transports ?

sections.label: Section 5.8.1035
sections.translation: Are you aware that my Pamphila has been discovered to be a citizen ?

sections.label: Section 5.8.1035b
sections.translation: I have heard so .

sections.label: Section 5.8.1035c
sections.translation: Do you know that she is betrothed to me ?

sections.label: Section 5.8.1036
sections.original: Bene , ita me Di ament , factum .
sections.translation: So may the Gods bless me , happily done .

sections.label: Section 5.8.1036b
sections.translation: Do you hear what he says ?

sections.label: Section 5.8.1036c
sections.translation: And then , besides , I am delighted that my brother ’ s mistress is secured to him ; the family is united . Thais has committed herself to the patronage of my father ; [*] she has put herself under our care and protection .

sections.label: Section 5.8.1039b
sections.translation: Thais , then , is wholly your brother ’ s .

sections.label: Section 5.8.1039c
sections.translation: Of course .

sections.label: Section 5.8.1040
sections.original: Iam hoc aliud est quod gaudeamus : miles pellitur foras .
sections.translation: Then this is another reason for us to rejoice , that the Captain will be beaten out of doors .

sections.label: Section 5.8.1041
sections.original: Tum tu frater , ubi ubi est , fac quam primum haec audiat .
sections.translation: Wherever my brother is , do you take care that he hears this as soon as possible .

sections.label: Section 5.8.1041b
sections.translation: I ’ ll go look for him at home .

sections.label: Section 5.8.1042
sections.original: Numquid , Gnatho , tu dubitas quin ego nunc perpetuo perierim ?
sections.translation: Do you at all doubt , Gnatho , but that I am now ruined everlastingly ?

sections.label: Section 5.8.1043
sections.translation: Without doubt , I do think so .

sections.label: Section 5.8.1043b
sections.original: Quid commemorem primum aut laudem maxime ?
sections.translation: What am I to make mention of first , or commend in especial ? Him who gave me the advice to do so , or myself , who ventured

sections.label: Section 5.8.1045
sections.original: Incipere ? an fortunam collaudem quae gubernatrix fuit ;
sections.translation: to undertake it ? Or ought I to extol fortune , who has been my guide , and has so opportunely crowded into a single day events so numerous , so important ; or my father ’ s kindness and indulgence Oh Jupiter , I entreat you , do preserve these blessings unto us !

sections.label: Section 5.8.1048b
sections.translation: Ye Gods , by our trust in you , what incredible things has Parmeno just related to me ! But where is my brother ?

sections.label: Section 5.8.1049b
sections.translation: Here he is .

sections.label: Section 5.8.1050
sections.translation: I ’ m overjoyed .

sections.label: Section 5.8.1050b
sections.original: Satis credo : nihil est Thaide hac , frater , tua
sections.translation: I quite believe you . There is no one , brother , more worthy to be loved than this Thais of yours : so much is she a benefactress to all our family .

sections.label: Section 5.8.1052
sections.translation: Whew ! are you commending her too to me ?

sections.label: Section 5.8.1052b
sections.original: Perii , quanto minus spei est , tanto magis amo .
sections.translation: I ’ m undone ; the less the hope I have , the more I am in love . Prithee , Gnatho , my hope is in you .

sections.label: Section 5.8.1053b
sections.translation: What do you wish me to do ?

sections.label: Section 5.8.1053c
sections.translation: Bring this about , by entreaties or with money , that I may at least share Thais ’ s favors in some degree .

sections.label: Section 5.8.1055
sections.translation: It ’ s a hard task .

sections.label: Section 5.8.1055b
sections.original: Si quid conlibuit , novi te : hoc si effeceris ,
sections.translation: If you set your mind on any thing , I know you well . If you manage this , ask me for any present you like as your reward ; you shall have what you ask .

sections.label: Section 5.8.1057
sections.translation: Is it so ?

sections.label: Section 5.8.1057b
sections.translation: It shall be so .

sections.label: Section 5.8.1057c
sections.original: Si efficio hoc , postulo ut mihi tua domus
sections.translation: If I manage this , I ask that your house , whether you are present or absent , may be open to me ; that , without invitation , there may always be a place for me .

sections.label: Section 5.8.1059b
sections.translation: I pledge my honor that it shall be so .

sections.label: Section 5.8.1059c
sections.translation: I ’ ll set about it then .

sections.label: Section 5.8.1059d
sections.translation: Who is it I hear so close at hand ? ( Turning round .) O Thraso —

sections.label: Section 5.8.1060b
sections.translation: Save you both —

sections.label: Section 5.8.1060c
sections.original: Tu fortasse quae facta hic sient
sections.translation: Perhaps you are not aware what has taken place here .

sections.label: Section 5.8.1061b
sections.translation: I am quite aware .

sections.label: Section 5.8.1061c
sections.original: Cur te ergo in his ego conspicor regionibus ?
sections.translation: Why , then , do I see you in this neighborhood ?

sections.label: Section 5.8.1062
sections.translation: Depending on your kindness .

sections.label: Section 5.8.1062b
sections.original: Scin quam fretus ? Miles , edico tibi ,
sections.translation: Do you know what sort of dependence you have ? Captain , I give you notice , if ever I catch you in this street again , even if you should say to me , I was looking for another person , I was on my road this way , you are undone .

sections.label: Section 5.8.1064b
sections.translation: Come , come , that ’ s not handsome .

sections.label: Section 5.8.1065
sections.translation: I ’ ve said it .

sections.label: Section 5.8.1065b
sections.translation: I didn ’ t know you gave yourself such airs .

sections.label: Section 5.8.1065c
sections.translation: So it shall be .

sections.label: Section 5.8.1066
sections.original: Prius audite paucis ; quod quum dixero , si placuerit ,
sections.translation: First hear a few words from me ; and when I have said the thing , if you approve of it , do it .

sections.label: Section 5.8.1067b
sections.translation: Let ’ s hear .
